summary: Intent handoff for the existing QwenPaw platform slices plus the strengthened P0 local security audit foundation. The next stage must materialize seven explicit security testcase entrypoints organized as four business scenarios and three deep-risk scenarios: end-to-end non-repudiation evidence chains, audit-integrity self-healing lock mode, tiered-audit TTFT balance, lease-expiry-driven active defense, prompt-injection-resistant tool enforcement, stolen-session replay blocking, and silent exfiltration pattern alerting. The implementation must continue to forbid explicit SecurityContext parameter threading, rely on contextvars-based implicit propagation across FastAPI and AgentScope message boundaries, write tamper-evident local audit records, anchor continuity through a restricted checkpoint store, verify configuration hashes during startup, separate synchronous L1 durability from asynchronous L2 reasoning audit writes, enforce high-risk policy at the tool-call boundary, asynchronously uplink critical audit anchors only after a successful pre-upload hash-alignment handshake, and let Security Center reconstruct evidence chains, detect replay, and surface abnormal low-risk aggregation behavior.

explicitTestcases.name: cli-version-surface
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Invoke qwenpaw --version through the click root entrypoint.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: Stdout contains the current package version and the command exits successfully.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: The root CLI version surface must keep exposing the installed package version without requiring any backend side effects.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize through the existing pytest node id tests/unit/cli/test_cli_version.py::test_cli_version_option_outputs_current_version.

explicitTestcases.name: agents-list-surface
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Invoke qwenpaw agents list with the shared agent tools helper monkeypatched to return one record.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: CLI stdout prints the helper-provided agent payload and exits successfully.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: The agents list surface must stay wired to the shared helper rather than duplicating agent listing logic inside the CLI shell.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize through the existing pytest node id tests/unit/cli/test_cli_agents.py::test_agents_list_uses_shared_tool_helper.

explicitTestcases.name: task-help-surface
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Invoke qwenpaw task --help through the click root entrypoint.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: Help output contains the stable task command flags and the command exits successfully.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: The task command must remain registered on the root CLI and preserve its documented operator-facing flag surface.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize through the existing pytest node id tests/unit/cli/test_cli_task.py::test_task_command_registered_in_cli.

explicitTestcases.name: settings-language-roundtrip
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Send PUT /api/settings/language and then GET /api/settings/language against the FastAPI router with a temporary settings file.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The GET response returns the language value persisted by the prior PUT request.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: The runtime settings router must keep the write and immediate read path consistent for language persistence.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize through the existing pytest node id tests/unit/routers/test_settings.py::test_put_then_get_roundtrip.

explicitTestcases.name: git-helper-command-runner
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Call qwenpaw.app.routers.git._git with run_command_async monkeypatched in a temporary path.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The helper delegates to the shared command runner with the expected command list and kwargs.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: The runtime git helper must keep using the shared command runner seam rather than embedding subprocess wiring directly in callers.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize through the existing pytest node id tests/unit/routers/test_git.py::test_git_helper_uses_shared_command_runner.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-024-end-to-end-non-repudiation-evidence-chain
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Authenticate as user A, route a high-risk business action through Agent B and third-party plugin C toward high-risk tool D, and provide the required user confirmation so the action can proceed.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: Security Center and the audit query surface reconstruct a continuous evidence chain from user A to Agent B to plugin C to tool D and bind it to the persisted confirmation artifact digest.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: The business acceptance boundary is full-chain non-repudiation rather than separate technical checks for context propagation and confirmation persistence. No visible link in the evidence chain may collapse into an anonymous or fallback identity.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize a new executable entrypoint at tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_end_to_end_non_repudiation_evidence_chain. This entrypoint replaces the prior split between implicit context propagation and high-risk confirmation recording.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-025-audit-integrity-self-healing-lockdown
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Execute a sensitive action, disconnect the device from the network, modify or truncate the local audit or settings store through operating-system means, then restart the client and attempt either reconnect or another sensitive action.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: Before the client resumes sensitive work or uploads new anchors, the local verifier and Security Center surface one business-visible data continuity anomaly and the local runtime enters lock mode.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: The implementation must collapse local tamper detection, out-of-band configuration tamper detection, and pre-uplink divergence into one closed-loop business outcome: the device is locked and the cloud console is alerted until continuity is re-established.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize a new executable entrypoint at tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_audit_integrity_self_healing_lockdown. This entrypoint replaces the prior split across local tamper, configuration tamper, and offline pre-uplink tamper cases.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-026-tiered-audit-ttft-balance
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Run the same operator-visible workflow once with critical-only audit and once with full audit plus cloud mirroring so authentication, tool execution, and reasoning all emit their expected audit records.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: Measured TTFT drift remains within five percent of the baseline while critical evidence remains durably persisted and L2 reasoning evidence stays available for later analysis.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: Full audit mode must preserve operator responsiveness. The five-percent TTFT budget remains frozen while the implementation still produces durable L1 evidence and analyzable L2 evidence.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize a new executable entrypoint at tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_tiered_audit_balances_ttft_within_budget. This entrypoint supersedes the earlier TTFT testcase name but preserves the same five-percent ceiling.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-027-lease-expiry-active-defense
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Allow a node to miss heartbeats long enough to exceed the lease TTL, observe Security Center mark it untrusted, then reconnect the device and attempt to recover model access without first completing gap validation.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The dashboard marks the device untrusted on lease expiry, reconnect does not restore authorization, and only a successful full-gap verification re-enables normal access.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: Lease expiry is an active defense outcome, not an informational warning. The device stays blocked until continuity is proved after return.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize a new executable entrypoint at tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_lease_expiry_blocks_untrusted_rejoin_until_gap_sync. This entrypoint replaces the prior lease-expiry testcase name while preserving the forced-gap-recovery rule.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-021-prompt-injection-tool-guard-enforced
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Inject nested or deceptive instructions that claim the agent is in test mode or otherwise exempt from approval, then drive the agent toward a tool whose configured risk level is High without providing valid trusted context or confirmation.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The high-risk tool remains blocked at the tool-call layer and the rejected attempt is recorded as a security-relevant event rather than executing.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: Prompt injection cannot redefine risk enforcement. Tool-layer guard evaluation remains authoritative even when conversation-level reasoning is compromised.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize a new executable entrypoint at tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_prompt_injection_cannot_bypass_high_risk_tool_guard and bind it to a malicious-prompt fixture plus tool-risk evaluation seam.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-022-session-hijack-replay-blocked
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Capture a valid session context after an authenticated action, inject it into a second unaudited process, and attempt to emit additional operations under the same trace or session identity.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: The cloud verifier detects that the replayed lineage does not match the next expected sequence and hash continuity, blocks further authorization for that session, and surfaces the attempted hijack in Security Center.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: Session continuity is trusted only when sequence and hash progression remain monotonic within the audited runtime. Stolen context replay from a shadow process must be blocked before more work is accepted.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize a new executable entrypoint at tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_stolen_session_replay_is_blocked_by_cloud_sequence_validation and bind it to cloud-side expected-sequence verification.

explicitTestcases.name: sec-e2e-023-silent-exfiltration-pattern-alerted
explicitTestcases.controlPoint: Run an agent workflow that repeatedly reads many small local files, searches across them, and summarizes results without invoking any tool currently marked High risk.
explicitTestcases.observationPoint: Security Center analyzes the emitted reasoning and access evidence, identifies the aggregated local sweep as abnormal behavior, and raises an operator-visible alert.
explicitTestcases.acceptanceBoundary: The business boundary is aggregate pattern detection rather than single-call policy enforcement. A covert low-risk data sweep must still become visible to operators.
explicitTestcases.implementationNotes: Materialize a new executable entrypoint at tests/integration/security/test_audit_foundation.py::test_silent_exfiltration_pattern_triggers_behavior_alert and bind it to L2 behavior analytics over emitted audit evidence.

frozenBaselines: prd/企业级个人助理平台安全基础设施设计方案 (基于 QwenPaw/AgentScope).md
notes: This handoff intentionally retires the prior explicit security testcase split across SEC-E2E-006, SEC-E2E-007, SEC-E2E-016, SEC-E2E-018, SEC-E2E-019, and SEC-E2E-020. The new frozen baseline is business-shaped: SEC-E2E-024 absorbs the former context-propagation and confirmation tests, SEC-E2E-025 absorbs the former local tamper, configuration tamper, and offline pre-uplink divergence tests, SEC-E2E-026 preserves the TTFT budget, and SEC-E2E-027 preserves lease-expiry active defense.

The implementation stage must materialize three new deep-risk entrypoints as first-class explicit baselines: SEC-E2E-021 for prompt-injection-resistant tool enforcement, SEC-E2E-022 for stolen-session replay blocking, and SEC-E2E-023 for silent exfiltration pattern alerting.

Control points and observation points are now intentionally expressed in business terms such as reconnect after offline tamper, submit malicious nested instructions, or replay a stolen session from a shadow process. The next stage should preserve that business framing rather than decomposing the scenarios back into code-centric subtests.

The cloud-edge collaboration model still depends on the same stable elements already in the graph: the local audit foundation emits durable and analyzable evidence, the uplink path performs handshake-first reconciliation, and Security Center reconstructs evidence chains, detects replay, and surfaces behavior anomalies.
The five-percent TTFT ceiling remains unchanged, and high-risk recovery remains blocked until continuity is revalidated after untrusted or replay-related failures.
